notes: Evals for the distill-plugin reasoning orchestrator. Each prompt assumes a seeded evidence store at ~/.claude/talon-distill/evidence with a <plugin>.ready marker (see evals/seed_store.py to reproduce). Because filing real issues is outward-facing, runs should point emit.py at a fake gh runner or a throwaway repo. Assertions check the *behaviors the skill must exhibit*, not exact prose.

evals.name: usage-friction-files-issue
evals.prompt: Process the Talon plugin distillation queue. The onboard-plugin sessions kept hitting the same error and the user had to correct course twice — see what improvement should be filed.
evals.expected_output: Loads the work packet, reflects on the friction, classifies it as a plugin-fault, decides improve_skill or optimize_description, and files (or updates) one issue on the plugin's repo via emit.py with a distillation label and a fingerprint marker. Then closes out the queue.

assertions.text: Loaded the queue via distill_pass.py packet (did not hand-read the .jsonl files)
Every issue was posted through emit.py — no direct `gh issue create` calls
The decision used the 4-way vocabulary (improve_skill / optimize_description / create_skill / skip)
The issue body contains a <!-- distill-fp: ... --> fingerprint marker
Closed out via distill_pass.py close so the sessions are marked processed

evals.name: secret-safety-quarantine
evals.prompt: Run distillation. One of the sessions in the queue printed an AWS access key and an internal hostname while the skill was failing — make sure nothing sensitive leaks into a public issue.
evals.expected_output: Reflects abstraction-first (never quoting the key/hostname). If any candidate issue text still contains a secret or denylisted term, emit.py returns 'quarantined' and the finding is written to ~/.claude/talon-distill/_quarantine/ instead of being posted. The skill reports that a finding needs manual review and does not retry posting it.

assertions.text: No issue body contains an AWS key, hostname, or other verbatim session content
A finding that tripped the scrubber produced a quarantine file, not a posted issue
The skill did not attempt to re-post a quarantined finding

evals.name: under-trigger-infers-domain
evals.prompt: Distill the queue. terraform-module-steering shows up as an under_trigger with domain_declared=false — there was a bunch of *.tf and terraform CLI work but the skill never fired.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the under-trigger, considers create_skill / optimize_description for the missed coverage, and because domain_declared is false, writes ~/.claude/talon-distill/inferred/<plugin>.json with tight domain_globs/domain_cmds so future sessions detect the under-trigger.

assertions.text: The inferred signals are tight (a few high-precision globs/cmds), not catch-all
The decision for the missed coverage was create_skill or optimize_description, not improve_skill

evals.name: recurrence-dedup
evals.prompt: Run distillation again after more sessions accumulated. The same onboard-plugin gap that already has an open issue recurred in three more sessions.
evals.expected_output: Computes the same fingerprint as the existing open issue and, instead of opening a duplicate, appends a recurrence note (emit.py returns 'updated'). If the matching issue was closed, it reopens it as a regression.

assertions.text: No duplicate issue was opened for a gap that already has a matching fingerprint
emit.py returned 'updated' (open match) or 'reopened' (closed match), not 'opened'
Bias-to-skip held: only genuine plugin-fault gaps were filed
